claim: The Union's hybrid-transitional coding may understate the sacrificial-escalation logic of total-war mobilization: conscription, mass casualties, and rhetoric of national rebirth through blood share structural features with high-pathology cases, complicating a clean hybrid reading.
rationale: The hybrid-transitional outcome distinguishes the Union from pure sacrificial-escalation cases, but the war period itself exhibits escalating sacrifice demands that may warrant a more complex periodized coding separating wartime (sacrificial-escalation) from Reconstruction (restrained-reordering).
